body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: bus+shim framing in ToO
body.intention: I intend that this ticket surfaces whether the ToO framing addition achieved its goal of reducing re-explanation load.
body.description:
**What changed:** ToO §1.3 and §2.1 now state the 'bus=network, shim=easy interface' framing explicitly.
**Predicted unintended effects:** The framing may conflict with the more mechanical description already in §1.3, creating two competing mental models for the same thing. Or the framing may be too abstract to stick without a concrete example.
**Signals to watch:** Does CC still ask for re-explanation of the bus/shim model in future sessions? Does Akien need to restate the framing? Does the ToO read coherently in one pass?
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-18 or after 2–3 sessions have used the updated ToO.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Ask Akien: 'Did the bus+shim framing in ToO reduce re-explanation? Did it land?' Annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close.
**Completion criteria:** Outcome annotated (occurred / clear / partial) and ticket closed.
